page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 16 vCPUs with 16 physical cores and 1 thread per core, operating at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 3200 GB NVMe SSD. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier. Large block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 340.0 GB/sec, and large block write memory bandwidth is recorded at 160.84 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is also in the top tier at 3133.97. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier. Redis SET operations perform at 38.03 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput is measured at 15.74 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU allocation. The shared CPU model represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, though the physical core count mitigates some multi-threading bottlenecks. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the 3200 GB NVMe storage, this configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose workloads requiring large local storage footprints.
description: The Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor on an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a bundled 3200 GB NVMe SSD. While the CPU allocation is shared, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and top-tier PassMark memory scores. Large block memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 340.0 GB/sec for reads and 160.84 GB/sec for writes. Multi-core CPU benchmarks and database operations sit in the average tier. This high-density memory and storage profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-optimized workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and applications requiring substantial local NVMe storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier memory performance for databases.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC Milan server featuring a high-capacity NVMe SSD and top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with 128.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 3200 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Shared CPU allocation suited for memory-intensive and database workloads
